name: Dal Bhat
description: The heart of every Nepali kitchen: warm lentil soup served over steamed rice. Comforting, wholesome, and made fresh almost every day.

steps.instruction: Rinse 1 cup of rice until the water runs clear, then cook it with 2 cups of water until soft.
steps.phrase: Great start, clean rice cooks up beautifully fluffy.

steps.instruction: Rinse 1 cup of lentils and add them to a pot with 3 cups of water, a pinch of turmeric, and salt.
steps.phrase: Take your time, the lentils just need a gentle simmer.

steps.instruction: Simmer the lentils for about 20 minutes until they turn soft and creamy.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, keep stirring now and then.

steps.instruction: In a small pan, heat 1 tablespoon of oil or ghee and fry cumin seeds, garlic, and dried chili.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Pour the sizzling tempering over the cooked lentils and stir gently.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, that aroma means flavour is locking in.

steps.instruction: Serve the warm dal over rice with a side of vegetables or pickle.
steps.phrase: Almost there, your homemade dal bhat is ready to enjoy.

name: Vegetable Momo
description: Soft steamed dumplings filled with spiced cabbage and carrots, a beloved Nepali street snack you can make at home.

steps.instruction: Mix 2 cups of flour with a little water and knead into a smooth, soft dough. Let it rest for 20 minutes.
steps.phrase: Great job, resting the dough makes it easy to roll.

steps.instruction: Finely chop cabbage, carrot, and onion, then mix with ginger, garlic, salt, and a little oil.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the colourful filling looks fresh and lovely.

steps.instruction: Roll the dough into small circles, keeping the edges thinner than the center.
steps.phrase: Take your time, even rounds make pretty momos.

steps.instruction: Place a spoon of filling in the middle and pleat the edges to seal each dumpling.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, your folds are getting neater each time.

steps.instruction: Arrange the momos in a greased steamer, leaving space between them.
steps.phrase: Almost there, give them a little room to breathe.

steps.instruction: Steam for 10 to 12 minutes until the wrappers turn glossy.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen smells wonderful, the momos are nearly ready.

steps.instruction: Serve hot with tomato achar or chili sauce.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your warm homemade momos.

name: Chicken Momo
description: Juicy minced chicken dumplings flavoured with ginger, garlic, and coriander. A festive favourite in Nepali homes.

steps.instruction: Knead 2 cups of flour with water into a soft dough and let it rest for 20 minutes.
steps.phrase: Great start, a rested dough is a happy dough.

steps.instruction: Mix minced chicken with chopped onion, ginger, garlic, coriander, salt, and a little oil.
steps.phrase: Take your time, gentle mixing keeps the filling tender.

steps.instruction: Roll the dough into thin circles with slightly thicker centers.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, your circles are coming along nicely.

steps.instruction: Add a spoon of chicken filling and pleat the edges to seal tightly.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, sealed momos hold all that tasty juice.

steps.instruction: Place the momos in a greased steamer with space around each one.
steps.phrase: Almost there, they just need a warm steam bath.

steps.instruction: Steam for 14 to 16 minutes until the chicken is fully cooked.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen smells amazing, the momos are nearly done.

steps.instruction: Serve hot with spicy tomato achar.
steps.phrase: Wonderful work, enjoy these juicy momos warm.

name: Thukpa
description: A hearty Himalayan noodle soup loaded with vegetables and warm spices, perfect for chilly evenings.

steps.instruction: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil and saute chopped onion, garlic, and ginger until soft.
steps.phrase: Great start, that base will make the soup sing.

steps.instruction: Add chopped tomatoes, turmeric, and a little chili powder, then cook into a soft masala.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Stir in sliced carrots, beans, and cabbage and cook for a few minutes.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the vegetables look perfect.

steps.instruction: Pour in 4 cups of water or broth and bring it to a boil.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the soup is taking shape.

steps.instruction: Add noodles and let them cook until soft, about 5 minutes.
steps.phrase: Take your time, soft noodles make a cozy bowl.

steps.instruction: Season with salt and finish with fresh coriander.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dish is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Ladle into bowls and serve piping hot.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, your warming thukpa is ready to enjoy.

name: Sel Roti
description: A sweet, ring-shaped rice bread fried until golden and crispy. A must-have during Tihar and other Nepali festivals.

steps.instruction: Soak 2 cups of rice for 4 to 6 hours, then drain well.
steps.phrase: Great start, patient soaking makes a smooth batter.

steps.instruction: Grind the rice with sugar, a little milk, and a pinch of cardamom into a thick, smooth batter.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, aim for a pourable but thick mix.

steps.instruction: Let the batter rest for 30 minutes so it settles nicely.
steps.phrase: Take your time, resting builds lovely flavour.

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a deep pan until hot but not smoking.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, steady heat gives a golden ring.

steps.instruction: Pour the batter in a circular motion to form a ring shape.
steps.phrase: You're doing wonderfully, your rings are taking form.

steps.instruction: Fry until golden brown on both sides, then lift out to drain.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen smells amazing, these look beautiful.

steps.instruction: Cool slightly and serve.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your crispy sel roti.

name: Aloo Tama
description: A tangy, comforting curry of potatoes and fermented bamboo shoots with black-eyed peas. Hearty and full of flavour.

steps.instruction: Soak a handful of black-eyed peas overnight, then boil until tender.
steps.phrase: Great start, soft beans cook into the curry beautifully.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and fry cumin seeds, onion, garlic, and ginger until golden.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add turmeric, chili powder, and chopped tomato, then cook into a soft masala.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, that masala base looks rich.

steps.instruction: Stir in cubed potatoes and the bamboo shoots and mix well.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the flavours are joining up.

steps.instruction: Add the boiled beans and enough water to make a thick gravy.
steps.phrase: Take your time, let everything get cozy together.

steps.instruction: Simmer for 15 minutes until the potatoes are soft and tangy.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dish is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Garnish with coriander and serve with rice.
steps.phrase: Wonderful work, your aloo tama is ready.

name: Gundruk ko Jhol
description: A tangy, soulful soup made from fermented leafy greens. A traditional Nepali comfort dish full of probiotic goodness.

steps.instruction: Soak a cup of gundruk in warm water for 10 minutes, then squeeze out the water.
steps.phrase: Great start, soaking softens the greens nicely.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and fry fenugreek seeds, garlic, and dried chili until fragrant.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell wonderfully earthy.

steps.instruction: Add the soaked gundruk and saute for a few minutes.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the greens are waking up with flavour.

steps.instruction: Stir in turmeric, a chopped tomato, and a few cubed potatoes.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the soup is building beautifully.

steps.instruction: Pour in 4 cups of water and bring to a gentle boil.
steps.phrase: Take your time, let the tang develop slowly.

steps.instruction: Simmer for 15 minutes until the potatoes are soft.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dish is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Season with salt and serve hot with rice.
steps.phrase: Lovely work, enjoy your warming gundruk soup.

name: Chatamari
description: Often called the Newari pizza, this thin rice-flour crepe is topped with egg, minced meat, or vegetables.

steps.instruction: Whisk rice flour with water and a pinch of salt into a smooth, pourable batter.
steps.phrase: Great start, a lump-free batter makes a tidy crepe.

steps.instruction: Heat a flat pan and lightly grease it with oil.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, a warm pan is ready for action.

steps.instruction: Pour a ladle of batter and spread it into a thin round.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, gentle spreading keeps it even.

steps.instruction: Sprinkle chopped onion, tomato, and your choice of topping over the batter.
steps.phrase: Take your time, arrange the toppings just how you like.

steps.instruction: Crack an egg on top if you wish and cover the pan to cook.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Cook until the base is crisp and the topping is set.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the chatamari is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Slide onto a plate and serve warm.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, enjoy your homemade Newari treat.

name: Chicken Choila
description: Smoky, spicy grilled chicken tossed with mustard oil, garlic, and fiery spices. A celebrated Newari favourite.

steps.instruction: Boil or grill chicken pieces until fully cooked, then shred into bite-sized strips.
steps.phrase: Great start, smoky char adds wonderful flavour.

steps.instruction: Heat mustard oil until it smokes lightly, then let it cool a little.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, warm mustard oil is the secret here.

steps.instruction: Mix the chicken with chili powder, turmeric, salt, garlic, and ginger.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, those spices look vibrant.

steps.instruction: Pour the warm mustard oil over the chicken and toss well.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add chopped onion, green chili, and fresh coriander.
steps.phrase: Take your time, fold everything in gently.

steps.instruction: Squeeze in a little lemon juice and mix once more.
steps.phrase: Almost there, that tang lifts the whole dish.

steps.instruction: Serve with beaten rice or as a spicy snack.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, your choila is ready to enjoy.

name: Chicken Sekuwa
description: Marinated chicken skewers grilled to smoky perfection, bursting with Himalayan spices. A popular street-style favourite.

steps.instruction: Cut chicken into bite-sized cubes and pat them dry.
steps.phrase: Great start, even pieces grill nice and evenly.

steps.instruction: Mix yogurt, ginger, garlic, cumin, chili, and mustard oil into a marinade.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, that marinade smells incredible already.

steps.instruction: Coat the chicken in the marinade and rest it for at least 1 hour.
steps.phrase: Take your time, marinating deepens the flavour.

steps.instruction: Thread the chicken onto skewers.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, your skewers look fantastic.

steps.instruction: Grill over charcoal or on a hot pan, turning often.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Cook until lightly charred and fully done inside.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dish is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Serve hot with onion rings and lemon.
steps.phrase: Wonderful work, enjoy your smoky sekuwa.

name: Kwati
description: A nourishing soup of nine sprouted beans, traditionally eaten during the Janai Purnima festival for strength and health.

steps.instruction: Soak a mix of nine beans overnight, then keep them covered for a day to sprout.
steps.phrase: Great start, sprouting brings out gentle goodness.

steps.instruction: Boil the sprouted beans with a little salt until soft.
steps.phrase: Take your time, tender beans make a creamy soup.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and fry cumin, ajwain, garlic, and ginger until fragrant.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add turmeric and chopped tomato and cook into a soft masala.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, that base looks rich and warm.

steps.instruction: Stir in the boiled beans and add enough water for a soup.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, everything is coming together.

steps.instruction: Simmer for 15 to 20 minutes to blend the flavours.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the soup is getting wonderfully hearty.

steps.instruction: Garnish with coriander and serve hot with rice.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your nourishing kwati.

name: Dhido
description: A traditional thick porridge made from buckwheat or millet flour, served with curry and greens. Simple, rustic, and filling.

steps.instruction: Boil 3 cups of water in a heavy pot.
steps.phrase: Great start, a steady boil makes mixing easier.

steps.instruction: Slowly add buckwheat or millet flour while stirring continuously.
steps.phrase: Take your time, slow stirring keeps it smooth.

steps.instruction: Keep mixing firmly with a wooden spoon as it thickens.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, this takes a little arm work.

steps.instruction: Cook until the dhido pulls away from the sides into a soft dough.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the texture is looking just right.

steps.instruction: Shape it gently with a spoon and place on a plate.
steps.phrase: Almost there, it is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Serve hot with gundruk soup, dal, or a vegetable curry.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, enjoy this hearty traditional meal.

name: Kheer
description: A creamy rice pudding gently simmered with milk, sugar, and cardamom, finished with nuts. A festive Nepali sweet.

steps.instruction: Rinse a quarter cup of rice and soak it for 15 minutes.
steps.phrase: Great start, soaked rice cooks soft and creamy.

steps.instruction: Bring 4 cups of milk to a gentle boil in a heavy pot.
steps.phrase: Take your time, slow heat stops the milk from sticking.

steps.instruction: Add the rice and simmer on low, stirring often.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, keep that gentle stir going.

steps.instruction: Cook until the rice is soft and the milk thickens.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell wonderfully sweet.

steps.instruction: Stir in sugar, cardamom, and chopped nuts.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the kheer is turning rich and creamy.

steps.instruction: Simmer a few more minutes until thick and luscious.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dessert is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Serve warm or chilled.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your homemade kheer.

name: Chicken Chowmein
description: Stir-fried noodles with chicken and crunchy vegetables in savoury sauces. A beloved everyday Nepali favourite.

steps.instruction: Boil the noodles until just soft, drain, and toss with a little oil.
steps.phrase: Great start, oiled noodles wont stick together.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and stir-fry thin chicken strips until cooked.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the chicken looks golden and tasty.

steps.instruction: Add sliced onion, carrot, cabbage, and beans and toss on high heat.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, keep the veggies crisp and bright.

steps.instruction: Pour in soy sauce, a little chili sauce, and salt.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add the noodles and toss everything together quickly.
steps.phrase: Take your time tossing so the sauce coats evenly.

steps.instruction: Stir-fry for a couple more minutes to mix the flavours.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dish is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Garnish with spring onion and serve hot.
steps.phrase: Wonderful work, enjoy your homemade chowmein.

name: Aloo ko Achar
description: A zesty potato salad tossed with sesame, lemon, and spices. A refreshing side that brightens any Nepali meal.

steps.instruction: Boil potatoes until soft, peel them, and cut into bite-sized cubes.
steps.phrase: Great start, soft potatoes soak up flavour beautifully.

steps.instruction: Dry-roast sesame seeds and grind them into a coarse powder.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, fresh sesame adds a nutty richness.

steps.instruction: Mix the potatoes with the sesame powder, turmeric, and chili powder.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the colours look lovely.

steps.instruction: Add lemon juice, salt, chopped green chili, and coriander.
steps.phrase: Take your time, taste and adjust the tang.

steps.instruction: Temper fenugreek seeds in hot oil and pour over the mix.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Toss everything well and let it rest a few minutes.
steps.phrase: Almost there, resting lets the flavours mingle.

steps.instruction: Serve as a side or a light snack.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your tangy aloo achar.

name: Bhatmas Sadeko
description: Crunchy fried soybeans tossed with onion, chili, and spices. A protein-rich snack often served with drinks or tea.

steps.instruction: Fry or roast soybeans until golden and crunchy.
steps.phrase: Great start, that crunch is what makes this snack.

steps.instruction: Let the soybeans cool slightly in a mixing bowl.
steps.phrase: Take your time, cooling keeps them nice and crisp.

steps.instruction: Add chopped onion, green chili, ginger, and coriander.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the mix is looking fresh and lively.

steps.instruction: Season with chili powder, salt, and a squeeze of lemon.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, that tang brings it alive.

steps.instruction: Drizzle a little mustard oil and toss everything together.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Serve right away while crunchy.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, enjoy your zesty bhatmas sadeko.

name: Rayo ko Saag
description: Tender mustard greens stir-fried with garlic and a hint of chili. A simple, healthy side loved in Nepali homes.

steps.instruction: Wash mustard greens well and chop them roughly.
steps.phrase: Great start, clean greens make a fresh dish.

steps.instruction: Heat mustard oil until lightly smoking, then lower the heat.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, mustard oil gives that classic taste.

steps.instruction: Add fenugreek seeds, garlic, and dried chili and fry briefly.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Toss in the chopped greens and stir on high heat.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the greens look vibrant.

steps.instruction: Add a pinch of turmeric and salt and cook until just wilted.
steps.phrase: Take your time, soft but bright greens are perfect.

steps.instruction: Serve hot with rice and dal.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your simple saag.

name: Khasi ko Masu
description: A rich, slow-cooked goat curry simmered with onions and warm Himalayan spices. A celebratory dish for special occasions.

steps.instruction: Wash goat pieces and marinate with turmeric, salt, and a little yogurt.
steps.phrase: Great start, marinating tenderises the meat.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and fry sliced onion until deep golden.
steps.phrase: Take your time, golden onions build deep flavour.

steps.instruction: Add ginger, garlic, and the marinated meat and sear well.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Stir in chili powder, cumin, coriander, and chopped tomato.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the masala is looking rich.

steps.instruction: Add water, cover, and simmer on low heat.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, slow cooking works its magic.

steps.instruction: Cook until the meat is fork-tender, about 45 minutes.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the curry is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Garnish with coriander and serve with rice.
steps.phrase: Wonderful work, enjoy this hearty goat curry.

name: Bara
description: Savoury lentil patties pan-fried until golden, a cherished Newari snack often topped with egg or minced meat.

steps.instruction: Soak black lentils for a few hours, then grind into a thick, smooth batter.
steps.phrase: Great start, a smooth batter fries up fluffy.

steps.instruction: Season the batter with salt, ginger, and a pinch of cumin.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, gentle seasoning lets the lentils shine.

steps.instruction: Heat a flat pan and grease it lightly with oil.
steps.phrase: Take your time, a warm pan helps it set.

steps.instruction: Spread a ladle of batter into a thick round.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, your rounds look neat.

steps.instruction: Cook until the base is golden, then flip carefully.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Crack an egg on top if you like and cook through.
steps.phrase: Almost there, it is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Serve warm with pickle or chutney.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your homemade bara.

name: Yomari
description: A steamed sweet dumpling shaped like a fig and filled with molasses and sesame. A festive Newari treat for Yomari Punhi.

steps.instruction: Knead rice flour with warm water into a soft, pliable dough.
steps.phrase: Great start, a soft dough shapes easily.

steps.instruction: Warm chaku or molasses with sesame and coconut into a thick filling.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell sweet and lovely.

steps.instruction: Take a ball of dough and shape it into a small cup.
steps.phrase: Take your time, gentle hands make pretty shapes.

steps.instruction: Fill the cup with the molasses mix and seal into a fig shape.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, those shapes look charming.

steps.instruction: Place the yomari in a greased steamer.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, they are ready for a warm steam.

steps.instruction: Steam for 15 minutes until the dough turns glossy.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dumplings are coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Cool slightly and enjoy warm.
steps.phrase: Wonderful work, enjoy your festive yomari.

name: Anda ko Tarkari
description: A comforting egg curry simmered in a spiced onion-tomato gravy. Quick, satisfying, and perfect with rice or roti.

steps.instruction: Boil the eggs, peel them, and lightly prick the surface.
steps.phrase: Great start, little pricks help the gravy soak in.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and fry the eggs until lightly golden, then set aside.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, golden eggs add lovely texture.

steps.instruction: In the same pan, saute onion, ginger, and garlic until soft.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add turmeric, chili, cumin, and chopped tomato and cook into a masala.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the gravy base looks rich.

steps.instruction: Pour in water for a light gravy and bring to a simmer.
steps.phrase: Take your time, let the flavours blend.

steps.instruction: Add the eggs back and simmer for 10 minutes.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the curry is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Garnish with coriander and serve with rice.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your egg curry.

name: Golbheda ko Achar
description: A smoky-sweet tomato pickle blended with sesame and spices. A tangy condiment that pairs perfectly with momos and rice.

steps.instruction: Roast tomatoes over a flame or in a pan until the skin chars.
steps.phrase: Great start, that smoky char adds magic.

steps.instruction: Peel the charred skin and mash the soft tomatoes.
steps.phrase: Take your time, peeling is easier when warm.

steps.instruction: Dry-roast sesame seeds and grind into a coarse powder.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, sesame gives a lovely nutty depth.

steps.instruction: Mix the mashed tomato with sesame, chili, garlic, and salt.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the colours look vibrant.

steps.instruction: Add a little lemon juice and fresh coriander.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Stir well and let it rest a few minutes.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the achar is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Serve as a dip or side.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, enjoy your tangy tomato achar.

name: Vegetable Pakora
description: Crispy fritters of mixed vegetables coated in spiced gram-flour batter. A favourite rainy-day tea-time snack.

steps.instruction: Slice onion, potato, and spinach into thin pieces.
steps.phrase: Great start, thin slices fry up extra crispy.

steps.instruction: Mix gram flour with turmeric, chili powder, salt, and water into a thick batter.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, a thick batter clings beautifully.

steps.instruction: Add the vegetables to the batter and coat them evenly.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, every piece looks well dressed.

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a deep pan until hot.
steps.phrase: Take your time, steady heat means even frying.

steps.instruction: Drop spoonfuls of the mix into the oil and fry until golden.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Lift out and drain on paper.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the pakoras look crisp and golden.

steps.instruction: Serve hot with chutney or ketchup.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your crunchy pakoras.

name: Sikarni
description: A rich, sweetened yogurt dessert flavoured with cardamom, saffron, and nuts. A creamy Newari treat for celebrations.

steps.instruction: Hang plain yogurt in a cloth for a few hours to drain the water.
steps.phrase: Great start, thick yogurt makes a luscious dessert.

steps.instruction: Transfer the thick yogurt into a bowl and whisk until smooth.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, a smooth base feels wonderfully creamy.

steps.instruction: Add sugar and whisk until fully dissolved.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, taste for the sweetness you like.

steps.instruction: Stir in cardamom powder and a pinch of saffron.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell wonderfully fragrant.

steps.instruction: Fold in chopped almonds, cashews, and pistachios.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dessert is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Chill for an hour and serve cold.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your cooling sikarni.

name: Vegetable Pulao
description: Fragrant rice cooked with mixed vegetables and gentle spices. A wholesome one-pot meal that comes together with ease.

steps.instruction: Rinse 1 cup of rice and soak it for 15 minutes, then drain.
steps.phrase: Great start, soaked rice cooks light and fluffy.

steps.instruction: Heat oil or ghee and fry cumin, bay leaf, and a cinnamon stick.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add chopped onion and saute until soft and golden.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, golden onions add gentle sweetness.

steps.instruction: Stir in mixed vegetables like peas, carrot, and beans.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the colours look lovely.

steps.instruction: Add the rice, salt, and 2 cups of water and bring to a boil.
steps.phrase: Take your time, let it reach a gentle bubble.

steps.instruction: Cover and cook on low heat until the rice is fluffy.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the pulao is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Fluff gently and serve warm.
steps.phrase: Wonderful work, enjoy your fragrant pulao.

name: Sukuti Sadeko
description: Spicy dried-meat salad tossed with onion, chili, and mustard oil. A chewy, flavour-packed snack to share.

steps.instruction: Roast or fry the dried meat until crisp, then cut into small pieces.
steps.phrase: Great start, crisp sukuti carries bold flavour.

steps.instruction: Let the meat cool slightly in a mixing bowl.
steps.phrase: Take your time, cooling keeps the texture pleasant.

steps.instruction: Add chopped onion, tomato, green chili, and coriander.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, fresh add-ins balance the richness.

steps.instruction: Season with chili powder, salt, and a squeeze of lemon.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, that tang brings it alive.

steps.instruction: Drizzle warm mustard oil and toss everything together.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Serve right away as a spicy snack.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your sukuti sadeko.

name: Mixed Vegetable Tarkari
description: A simple home-style curry of seasonal vegetables in a light spiced gravy. Wholesome, colourful, and easy to make.

steps.instruction: Chop a mix of potato, cauliflower, beans, and carrot into even pieces.
steps.phrase: Great start, even pieces cook at the same pace.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and fry cumin seeds, onion, garlic, and ginger.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add turmeric, chili powder, and chopped tomato and cook into a masala.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the base is looking rich.

steps.instruction: Stir in the vegetables and coat them in the spices.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, everything looks colourful.

steps.instruction: Add a little water, cover, and simmer until tender.
steps.phrase: Take your time, soft veggies make a lovely curry.

steps.instruction: Season with salt and finish with coriander.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dish is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Serve hot with rice or roti.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, enjoy your homely vegetable tarkari.

name: Makai ko Roti
description: A rustic cornmeal flatbread with a lovely golden crust. Simple, hearty, and naturally gluten-free.

steps.instruction: Mix cornmeal with a pinch of salt and warm water into a soft dough.
steps.phrase: Great start, warm water makes the dough easy to shape.

steps.instruction: Knead gently until the dough holds together.
steps.phrase: Take your time, cornmeal dough is a little delicate.

steps.instruction: Divide into balls and pat each into a flat round with your hands.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, your rounds look lovely.

steps.instruction: Heat a flat pan over medium heat.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, steady heat gives a golden crust.

steps.instruction: Cook each roti, pressing gently, until both sides are browned.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Serve warm with greens, curry, or pickle.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your makai ko roti.

name: Mula ko Achar
description: A crunchy, tangy radish pickle with mustard and fenugreek. A refreshing condiment that wakes up any plate.

steps.instruction: Grate or thinly slice fresh radish and sprinkle with a little salt.
steps.phrase: Great start, salt draws out extra moisture.

steps.instruction: Let the radish sit for 10 minutes, then gently squeeze it.
steps.phrase: Take your time, this keeps the pickle nice and crisp.

steps.instruction: Dry-roast and grind fenugreek seeds into a fine powder.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, fenugreek adds a warm aroma.

steps.instruction: Mix the radish with turmeric, chili powder, and the fenugreek.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the colours look bright.

steps.instruction: Add lemon juice and a drizzle of warm mustard oil.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Toss well and let it rest before serving.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your crunchy radish achar.

name: Masala Bhuteko Aloo
description: Spiced stir-fried potatoes with cumin and turmeric, crisp at the edges and soft inside. A quick everyday favourite.

steps.instruction: Boil potatoes until just tender, peel, and cut into cubes.
steps.phrase: Great start, par-boiled potatoes fry up beautifully.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and crackle cumin seeds and a dried chili.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add the potatoes and toss to coat them in the oil.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, they are starting to sizzle.

steps.instruction: Sprinkle turmeric, chili powder, and salt over the potatoes.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the spices are clinging on lovely.

steps.instruction: Stir-fry until the edges turn golden and crisp.
steps.phrase: Take your time, crispy edges are worth the wait.

steps.instruction: Finish with coriander and serve hot.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your spiced potatoes.

name: Luchi
description: Soft, puffy deep-fried flatbreads made from white flour. A festive Bengali breakfast favourite, light and pillowy.

steps.instruction: Mix flour with a pinch of salt and a spoon of oil, then knead with water into a soft dough.
steps.phrase: Great start, a smooth dough makes pillowy luchis.

steps.instruction: Cover the dough and let it rest for 15 minutes.
steps.phrase: Take your time, resting relaxes the dough.

steps.instruction: Divide into small balls and roll each into a thin round.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, even rounds puff up best.

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a deep pan until hot.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, hot oil helps them balloon.

steps.instruction: Slide a luchi in and gently press until it puffs.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Flip once and fry until pale golden, then drain.
steps.phrase: Almost there, look at those lovely puffs.

steps.instruction: Serve warm with aloo dum or a curry.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your fluffy luchis.

name: Bengali Aloo Dum
description: Baby potatoes simmered in a rich, lightly sweet-spiced tomato gravy. The classic partner for luchi.

steps.instruction: Boil baby potatoes until tender, peel, and prick them gently.
steps.phrase: Great start, pricked potatoes drink up the gravy.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and lightly fry the potatoes until golden, then set aside.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, golden potatoes add lovely depth.

steps.instruction: In the same oil, fry cumin, bay leaf, and ginger paste.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add tomato puree, turmeric, chili, and a pinch of sugar.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the gravy is turning rich.

steps.instruction: Return the potatoes and add a little water.
steps.phrase: Take your time, let them soak up the spices.

steps.instruction: Simmer until the gravy thickens and coats the potatoes.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dish is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, enjoy your cozy aloo dum.

name: Chingri Malai Curry
description: Plump prawns simmered in a fragrant coconut-milk gravy. A creamy, festive Bengali delicacy fit for celebrations.

steps.instruction: Clean and devein the prawns, then toss with turmeric and salt.
steps.phrase: Great start, well-cleaned prawns taste sweet and fresh.

steps.instruction: Lightly fry the prawns until they just turn pink, then set aside.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, do not overcook so they stay tender.

steps.instruction: In the same pan, fry bay leaf, cardamom, and ginger paste.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add onion paste and cook until soft and pale golden.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, a smooth base makes a creamy curry.

steps.instruction: Pour in coconut milk with a pinch of sugar and salt.
steps.phrase: Take your time, gentle heat keeps the coconut silky.

steps.instruction: Return the prawns and simmer for a few minutes.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the curry is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Serve warm with steamed rice.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your creamy chingri malai.

name: Shorshe Ilish
description: Hilsa fish bathed in a pungent mustard-seed gravy. An iconic Bengali dish celebrating the beloved monsoon fish.

steps.instruction: Soak mustard seeds for 15 minutes, then grind with green chili into a paste.
steps.phrase: Great start, fresh mustard paste is the soul of this dish.

steps.instruction: Marinate the hilsa pieces with turmeric and salt.
steps.phrase: Take your time, gentle handling keeps the fish whole.

steps.instruction: Heat mustard oil and add nigella seeds and slit green chilies.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell wonderfully sharp.

steps.instruction: Mix the mustard paste with turmeric and a little water.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the gravy is taking on a golden hue.

steps.instruction: Add the fish and pour the mustard gravy over it.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the fish looks beautifully coated.

steps.instruction: Simmer gently for 8 to 10 minutes.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dish is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Drizzle a little raw mustard oil and serve with rice.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, enjoy your classic shorshe ilish.

name: Mishti Doi
description: Sweet, caramel-toned set yogurt with a smooth, custard-like texture. A beloved Bengali dessert in earthen pots.

steps.instruction: Gently heat milk and reduce it slightly until thickened.
steps.phrase: Great start, reducing milk builds a rich body.

steps.instruction: In another pan, melt sugar until it turns golden caramel.
steps.phrase: Take your time, slow caramel gives the lovely colour.

steps.instruction: Stir the caramel into the warm milk until dissolved.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the milk is taking on a warm hue.

steps.instruction: Let the milk cool until just warm to the touch.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the right warmth helps it set.

steps.instruction: Whisk in a few spoons of yogurt as a starter.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to feel like a sweet shop.

steps.instruction: Pour into pots, cover, and keep in a warm place to set.
steps.phrase: Almost there, it just needs patience to firm up.

steps.instruction: Chill before serving.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your silky mishti doi.

name: Sandesh
description: Delicate fresh-cheese sweets flavoured with cardamom. A refined Bengali dessert that melts in the mouth.

steps.instruction: Boil milk and add lemon juice to curdle it into soft chenna.
steps.phrase: Great start, gentle curdling gives soft cheese.

steps.instruction: Strain the chenna in a cloth and rinse to remove sourness.
steps.phrase: Take your time, rinsing keeps the flavour clean.

steps.instruction: Knead the chenna with your palm until smooth and lump-free.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, smooth chenna makes silky sandesh.

steps.instruction: Cook the chenna with sugar on low heat for a few minutes.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell sweet and lovely.

steps.instruction: Mix in cardamom and let the mixture cool a little.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the fragrance is wonderful.

steps.instruction: Shape into small rounds or press into moulds.
steps.phrase: Almost there, your sweets are taking pretty shapes.

steps.instruction: Garnish with a pistachio and serve.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your delicate sandesh.

name: Rasgulla
description: Spongy chenna balls simmered in light sugar syrup. A juicy, melt-in-the-mouth Bengali classic.

steps.instruction: Boil milk and curdle it with lemon juice to make chenna.
steps.phrase: Great start, fresh chenna is the secret to softness.

steps.instruction: Strain and rinse the chenna, then squeeze out extra water.
steps.phrase: Take your time, the right moisture matters here.

steps.instruction: Knead the chenna until smooth, then roll into small balls.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, smooth balls puff up beautifully.

steps.instruction: Boil sugar with water to make a light syrup.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, a thin syrup lets them swell.

steps.instruction: Gently drop the balls into the boiling syrup.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell sweet.

steps.instruction: Cover and cook until the balls double in size.
steps.phrase: Almost there, watch them grow soft and spongy.

steps.instruction: Cool in the syrup and serve chilled.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your juicy rasgullas.

name: Kosha Mangsho
description: Slow-cooked mutton in a deeply spiced, glossy onion gravy. A rich Bengali Sunday-special loved with luchi or rice.

steps.instruction: Marinate mutton with yogurt, turmeric, ginger-garlic, and salt for an hour.
steps.phrase: Great start, a good marinade tenderises the meat.

steps.instruction: Heat mustard oil and fry sliced onion until deep golden.
steps.phrase: Take your time, golden onions build deep flavour.

steps.instruction: Add the marinated mutton and sear on high heat.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Stir in chili, cumin, coriander, and a pinch of sugar.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the masala is turning rich and dark.

steps.instruction: Cover and cook slowly, stirring often, with little water.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, low and slow is the key.

steps.instruction: Cook until the meat is tender and the gravy clings to it.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dish is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Finish with garam masala and serve hot.
steps.phrase: Wonderful work, enjoy your rich kosha mangsho.

name: Cholar Dal
description: Bengal gram cooked into a mildly sweet, fragrant lentil dish with coconut and warm spices. A festive comfort dish.

steps.instruction: Rinse chana dal and boil with turmeric until soft but not mushy.
steps.phrase: Great start, tender dal makes a lovely texture.

steps.instruction: Heat ghee and fry cumin, bay leaf, and a cinnamon stick.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add small coconut pieces and fry until lightly golden.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the coconut adds a sweet crunch.

steps.instruction: Pour in the cooked dal with a pinch of sugar and salt.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the dish is balancing sweet and savoury.

steps.instruction: Simmer gently until the flavours blend together.
steps.phrase: Take your time, slow simmering deepens the taste.

steps.instruction: Finish with a sprinkle of garam masala.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dal is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Serve warm with luchi or rice.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your fragrant cholar dal.

name: Beguni
description: Thinly sliced eggplant dipped in spiced gram-flour batter and fried crisp. A favourite Bengali tea-time fritter.

steps.instruction: Slice the eggplant into thin long pieces.
steps.phrase: Great start, thin slices fry up wonderfully crisp.

steps.instruction: Mix gram flour with turmeric, chili, salt, and water into a smooth batter.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, a smooth batter coats evenly.

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a deep pan until hot.
steps.phrase: Take your time, the right heat gives a golden crust.

steps.instruction: Dip each eggplant slice into the batter to coat it.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, every slice looks well dressed.

steps.instruction: Fry the slices until crisp and golden on both sides.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Drain on paper and serve hot.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your crunchy beguni.

name: Bhetki Paturi
description: Fish fillets coated in mustard-coconut paste, wrapped in banana leaf, and steamed. A fragrant Bengali delicacy.

steps.instruction: Marinate the fish fillets with turmeric and salt.
steps.phrase: Great start, a gentle marinade keeps the fish moist.

steps.instruction: Grind mustard seeds, coconut, and green chili into a smooth paste.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, this paste gives a wonderful aroma.

steps.instruction: Coat the fish thoroughly with the mustard-coconut paste.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the fish is beautifully dressed.

steps.instruction: Place each piece on a banana leaf and wrap into a parcel.
steps.phrase: Take your time, neat parcels hold the flavour in.

steps.instruction: Steam the parcels for 12 to 15 minutes.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Unwrap carefully and serve with rice.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your fragrant paturi.

name: Labra
description: A mixed seasonal vegetable medley gently spiced and slow-cooked. A wholesome Bengali dish often made during festivals.

steps.instruction: Chop a mix of pumpkin, potato, radish, and beans into chunks.
steps.phrase: Great start, a colourful mix makes a hearty dish.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and crackle panch phoron and a dried red chili.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add the vegetables and toss them in the spices.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, everything is sizzling nicely.

steps.instruction: Sprinkle turmeric, salt, and a little sugar over the mix.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, that balance is classic Bengali.

steps.instruction: Cover and cook on low heat until the vegetables turn soft.
steps.phrase: Take your time, slow cooking blends the flavours.

steps.instruction: Mash lightly so it holds together.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the labra is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Serve warm with rice or khichuri.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, enjoy your homely labra.

name: Payesh
description: A creamy Bengali rice pudding slow-cooked in milk with jaggery or sugar and cardamom. A sweet for birthdays and festivals.

steps.instruction: Rinse a handful of rice and soak it for 15 minutes.
steps.phrase: Great start, soaked rice turns soft and creamy.

steps.instruction: Bring milk to a gentle boil in a heavy-bottomed pot.
steps.phrase: Take your time, slow heat keeps milk from sticking.

steps.instruction: Add the rice and simmer on low, stirring often.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, keep that gentle stir going.

steps.instruction: Cook until the rice is soft and the milk thickens.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell wonderfully sweet.

steps.instruction: Stir in jaggery or sugar and cardamom.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the payesh is turning rich.

steps.instruction: Simmer a few more minutes and add nuts.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dessert is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Serve warm or chilled.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your creamy payesh.

name: Macher Jhol
description: A light, soupy fish curry with potatoes and gentle spices. An everyday Bengali staple, comforting and clean-tasting.

steps.instruction: Marinate fish pieces with turmeric and salt, then lightly fry them.
steps.phrase: Great start, a light fry helps the fish hold shape.

steps.instruction: In the same oil, fry nigella seeds and green chilies.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add potato wedges, turmeric, and a little cumin.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the base is building gently.

steps.instruction: Pour in water and bring to a simmer until the potatoes soften.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the broth is coming together.

steps.instruction: Slide in the fried fish and simmer for a few minutes.
steps.phrase: Take your time, gentle cooking keeps fish tender.

steps.instruction: Finish with coriander and serve with rice.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your comforting macher jhol.

name: Aloo Posto
description: Potatoes cooked in a creamy poppy-seed paste with green chilies. A simple, soul-soothing Bengali classic.

steps.instruction: Soak poppy seeds in warm water, then grind into a smooth paste.
steps.phrase: Great start, a fine paste makes a creamy dish.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and add nigella seeds and a green chili.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add cubed potatoes and saute for a few minutes.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the potatoes are softening nicely.

steps.instruction: Stir in the poppy-seed paste with a little turmeric and salt.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the paste coats everything beautifully.

steps.instruction: Add a splash of water, cover, and cook until tender.
steps.phrase: Take your time, low heat keeps the paste smooth.

steps.instruction: Drizzle a little mustard oil and serve with rice.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your creamy aloo posto.

name: Shukto
description: A gently bitter-sweet mixed vegetable stew with milk and a hint of mustard. A traditional Bengali starter for the meal.

steps.instruction: Chop bitter gourd, drumstick, eggplant, and potato into pieces.
steps.phrase: Great start, a balanced mix is key to shukto.

steps.instruction: Lightly fry the bitter gourd until golden and set aside.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, frying softens its bitterness.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and crackle panch phoron and a bay leaf.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add the remaining vegetables and saute gently.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the veggies look lovely.

steps.instruction: Pour in a little milk with ginger paste and a pinch of sugar.
steps.phrase: Take your time, the milk gives a gentle creaminess.

steps.instruction: Simmer until the vegetables are soft, then return the gourd.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the stew is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Serve warm at the start of a meal with rice.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, enjoy your delicate shukto.

name: Begun Bhaja
description: Eggplant rounds dusted with turmeric and chili, then shallow-fried until soft and golden. A simple, beloved side.

steps.instruction: Slice the eggplant into round pieces.
steps.phrase: Great start, even rounds fry up beautifully.

steps.instruction: Rub the slices with turmeric, chili powder, and salt.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the spices add lovely colour.

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a flat pan over medium heat.
steps.phrase: Take your time, steady heat avoids burning.

steps.instruction: Place the slices in the pan and fry until golden.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Flip carefully and cook the other side.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, they look soft and golden.

steps.instruction: Drain and serve hot with rice and dal.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your begun bhaja.

name: Dhokar Dalna
description: Spiced lentil cakes fried and simmered in a light gravy. A cherished vegetarian Bengali main, hearty and flavourful.

steps.instruction: Soak chana dal, then grind into a thick paste with ginger and chili.
steps.phrase: Great start, a firm paste sets into neat cakes.

steps.instruction: Cook the paste with spices until it thickens, then spread on a tray.
steps.phrase: Take your time, let it cool and firm up well.

steps.instruction: Cut into diamond shapes and fry until golden, then set aside.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, those little cakes look lovely.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and fry cumin, bay leaf, and ginger paste.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add tomato, turmeric, and chili and cook into a gravy.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the gravy is building nicely.

steps.instruction: Add water, simmer, then gently add the fried cakes.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dish is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Simmer briefly and serve with rice.
steps.phrase: Wonderful work, enjoy your dhokar dalna.

name: Basanti Pulao
description: A fragrant golden sweet pulao with raisins, nuts, and saffron. A festive Bengali rice dish for special occasions.

steps.instruction: Rinse basmati rice and soak it for 20 minutes, then drain.
steps.phrase: Great start, soaked rice cooks light and separate.

steps.instruction: Mix the rice with turmeric, a little ghee, and salt, and rest it.
steps.phrase: Take your time, this gives the lovely golden colour.

steps.instruction: Heat ghee and fry cardamom, cinnamon, and bay leaf.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add cashews and raisins and fry until lightly golden.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the nuts add a festive touch.

steps.instruction: Stir in the rice and add measured warm water with a little sugar.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the balance of sweet is gentle.

steps.instruction: Cover and cook on low heat until the rice is fluffy.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the pulao is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Fluff gently and serve warm.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your golden basanti pulao.

name: Bengali Chicken Curry
description: A homely chicken curry in a spiced onion-tomato gravy with potatoes. Comforting, everyday goodness on the dinner table.

steps.instruction: Marinate chicken pieces with yogurt, turmeric, and salt.
steps.phrase: Great start, marinating keeps the chicken juicy.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and fry potato chunks until golden, then set aside.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, fried potatoes add a lovely touch.

steps.instruction: Fry sliced onion until soft, then add ginger-garlic paste.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add chili, cumin, coriander, and chopped tomato and cook into a masala.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the gravy is turning rich.

steps.instruction: Add the chicken and sear, then add water and the potatoes.
steps.phrase: Take your time, let it all simmer together.

steps.instruction: Cook until the chicken is tender and the gravy thickens.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the curry is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Finish with garam masala and serve with rice.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, enjoy your homely chicken curry.

name: Doi Maach
description: Fish simmered in a smooth, lightly spiced yogurt gravy. A delicate, tangy Bengali curry with gentle warmth.

steps.instruction: Marinate fish with turmeric and salt, then lightly fry it.
steps.phrase: Great start, a gentle fry keeps the fish firm.

steps.instruction: Whisk yogurt until smooth and lump-free.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, smooth yogurt makes a silky gravy.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and fry cardamom, cinnamon, and ginger paste.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Lower the heat and stir in the whisked yogurt slowly.
steps.phrase: Take your time, low heat keeps the yogurt from splitting.

steps.instruction: Add a pinch of sugar, salt, and a little water.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the gravy is coming together.

steps.instruction: Slide in the fried fish and simmer gently.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the curry is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Serve warm with steamed rice.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your tangy doi maach.

name: Ghugni
description: Yellow peas simmered in a spiced gravy and topped with onion and coriander. A popular Bengali street snack.

steps.instruction: Soak dried yellow peas overnight, then boil until soft.
steps.phrase: Great start, well-soaked peas cook up tender.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and fry cumin, bay leaf, and chopped onion.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add ginger, turmeric, chili, and chopped tomato and cook into a masala.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the base is looking rich.

steps.instruction: Stir in the boiled peas with a little of their water.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, everything is blending nicely.

steps.instruction: Simmer until the gravy thickens and coats the peas.
steps.phrase: Take your time, a thick gravy is just right.

steps.instruction: Sprinkle roasted cumin, chopped onion, and coriander.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dish is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Serve warm as a snack.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, enjoy your hearty ghugni.

name: Mochar Ghonto
description: A spiced, dry banana-flower preparation with coconut and gentle warmth. A traditional, nutritious Bengali dish.

steps.instruction: Clean and finely chop the banana flower, then boil it until soft.
steps.phrase: Great start, soft florets are easy to cook with.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and crackle cumin, bay leaf, and dried chili.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add ginger paste, turmeric, and a little cumin powder.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the spices are blooming nicely.

steps.instruction: Stir in the boiled banana flower and mix well.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, everything is coming together.

steps.instruction: Add grated coconut, a pinch of sugar, and salt.
steps.phrase: Take your time, the coconut adds lovely sweetness.

steps.instruction: Cook on low heat until dry and fragrant.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dish is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Serve warm with rice.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your mochar ghonto.

name: Aloo Phulkopir Dalna
description: Potatoes and cauliflower in a comforting spiced gravy. A simple, warming Bengali winter favourite.

steps.instruction: Cut cauliflower into florets and potatoes into chunks.
steps.phrase: Great start, even pieces cook at the same pace.

steps.instruction: Lightly fry the florets and potatoes until golden, then set aside.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, frying adds a lovely depth.

steps.instruction: Heat oil and fry cumin, bay leaf, and ginger paste.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add turmeric, chili, and chopped tomato and cook into a masala.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the gravy is building nicely.

steps.instruction: Return the vegetables and add a little water.
steps.phrase: Take your time, let them soak up the spices.

steps.instruction: Simmer until the vegetables are tender and the gravy thickens.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the dish is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Finish with garam masala and serve with rice or luchi.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, enjoy your cozy dalna.

name: Narkel Naru
description: Sweet coconut laddus bound with jaggery or sugar. A simple, traditional Bengali festive treat.

steps.instruction: Grate fresh coconut finely.
steps.phrase: Great start, fresh coconut gives the best flavour.

steps.instruction: Melt jaggery or sugar gently in a pan.
steps.phrase: Take your time, slow melting avoids burning.

steps.instruction: Add the grated coconut and stir continuously.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell wonderfully sweet.

steps.instruction: Cook until the mixture thickens and pulls together.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, watch it come together.

steps.instruction: Add a pinch of cardamom and let it cool slightly.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, that aroma is lovely.

steps.instruction: Roll the warm mixture into small round balls.
steps.phrase: Almost there, your naru are taking shape.

steps.instruction: Cool fully and serve.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your coconut naru.

name: Patishapta
description: Thin crepes rolled around a sweet coconut or khoya filling. A beloved Bengali treat made during the Poush Sankranti festival.

steps.instruction: Mix rice flour, flour, and a little semolina with milk into a thin batter.
steps.phrase: Great start, a thin batter makes delicate crepes.

steps.instruction: Cook grated coconut with jaggery until thick to make the filling.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell wonderfully sweet.

steps.instruction: Heat a flat pan and lightly grease it.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, a warm pan is ready.

steps.instruction: Pour a ladle of batter and spread it into a thin oval.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, gentle spreading keeps it even.

steps.instruction: Place a line of filling on one side and roll the crepe.
steps.phrase: Take your time, careful rolling keeps it neat.

steps.instruction: Cook until set, then lift out gently.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the crepes look lovely.

steps.instruction: Serve warm.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your festive patishapta.

name: Tomato Khejur Chutney
description: A sweet-tangy tomato chutney with dates and raisins. A festive Bengali condiment served at the end of a meal.

steps.instruction: Chop the tomatoes and soak a few dates and raisins.
steps.phrase: Great start, soaked fruit turns soft and sweet.

steps.instruction: Heat a little oil and crackle panch phoron and a dried chili.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add the tomatoes with turmeric and a pinch of salt.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, the tomatoes will soon turn jammy.

steps.instruction: Cook until the tomatoes soften and break down.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, keep stirring gently.

steps.instruction: Add sugar, dates, and raisins and simmer.
steps.phrase: Take your time, the sweetness develops slowly.

steps.instruction: Cook until thick and glossy.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the chutney is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Cool and serve as a sweet side.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your tangy-sweet chutney.

name: Dimer Dalna
description: Boiled eggs fried golden and simmered in a spiced potato gravy. A homely, satisfying Bengali egg curry.

steps.instruction: Boil the eggs, peel them, and prick the surface gently.
steps.phrase: Great start, little pricks help them soak up gravy.

steps.instruction: Fry the eggs with a little turmeric until golden, then set aside.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, golden eggs look and taste lovely.

steps.instruction: Fry potato chunks until golden in the same oil.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the potatoes add heartiness.

steps.instruction: Add onion, ginger, turmeric, chili, and tomato and cook into a masala.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add water and the potatoes, then simmer until tender.
steps.phrase: Take your time, let the gravy develop.

steps.instruction: Return the eggs and simmer for a few minutes.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the curry is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Finish with garam masala and serve with rice.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, enjoy your dimer dalna.

name: Radhaballavi
description: Soft fried breads stuffed with a spiced lentil filling. A festive Bengali favourite, best paired with aloo dum.

steps.instruction: Soak urad dal, then grind into a coarse paste with ginger and chili.
steps.phrase: Great start, a coarse paste makes a tasty filling.

steps.instruction: Cook the dal paste with spices until dry and crumbly, then cool.
steps.phrase: Take your time, a dry filling stuffs more easily.

steps.instruction: Knead flour with a little oil and water into a soft dough.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, a soft dough rolls out smoothly.

steps.instruction: Stuff a ball of dough with the filling and seal it well.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, gentle sealing prevents leaks.

steps.instruction: Roll out gently into a small round.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is getting ready for a treat.

steps.instruction: Deep-fry until puffed and golden on both sides.
steps.phrase: Almost there, look at those lovely puffs.

steps.instruction: Serve hot with aloo dum.
steps.phrase: Beautifully done, enjoy your radhaballavi.

name: Chanar Dalna
description: Fresh paneer-style cheese cubes simmered in a fragrant potato gravy. A festive vegetarian Bengali main.

steps.instruction: Curdle milk to make chenna, press it firm, and cut into cubes.
steps.phrase: Great start, firm chenna holds its shape in the gravy.

steps.instruction: Lightly fry the chenna cubes until golden, then set aside.
steps.phrase: Nicely done, golden cubes add a lovely bite.

steps.instruction: Fry potato chunks until golden in the same oil.
steps.phrase: You're doing great, the potatoes will enrich the dish.

steps.instruction: Add cumin, ginger, turmeric, chili, and tomato and cook into a masala.
steps.phrase: Your kitchen is starting to smell amazing.

steps.instruction: Add water and the potatoes, then simmer until tender.
steps.phrase: Take your time, let the gravy come together.

steps.instruction: Gently add the chenna cubes and simmer briefly.
steps.phrase: Almost there, the curry is coming together beautifully.

steps.instruction: Finish with garam masala and serve with rice or luchi.
steps.phrase: Wonderful, enjoy your chanar dalna.
